author: Mike H
content: Would love a blog post/example on how you can have the client manage a successful request/response using a message queue that doesn’t provide message persistence or delivery guarantees. RabbitMQ and Kafka work well in they can (arguably) be considered a database; the client publishes a message and trusts the thing that needs to happen will happen. Retry mechanisms/persistence/etc in message systems allow this to be true. I’m all for ‘do one thing and do it well’, would love to see how the responsibility shift to the client works so larger systems can be built on NATS.
